Snippet 1: In response to environmental stress, eukaryotic cells reversibly form functional amyloid aggregates called amyloid bodies (A-bodies). This indicates that proteins can aggregate into amyloids as part of a functional process.

Snippet 8: These DNA-mediated changes in assembly kinetics and pathway selection suggest a regulatory mechanism for achieving structural and functional diversity in nonpathological amyloid systems. This indicates functional diversity.

Snippet 9: Our findings provide a molecular framework for understanding nucleic acid-guided amyloidogenesis and highlight how functional amyloids may exploit multiple assembly routes to fulfill their physiological roles. So amyloids can have multiple roles.

Snippet 10: Amyloids, once viewed solely as pathological hallmarks, are now recognized as widespread and versatile functional protein assemblies. So they are versatile.
